div.head: The American Adviser (Mayer) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 960. AghnidesThanassis Aghnides, Director of the Disarmament Section of the League of Nations. states HendersonArthur Henderson, President of the Disarmament Conference. approves committees meeting the first days of February. Aghnides also says there is a “gentleman’s agreement” among chairmen of the three committeesSpecial Committee for the Regulation of Trade in and the Private and State Manufacture of Arms; Technical Committee Dealing With the National Defense Expenditure Commission; Committee on Miscellaneous Provisions. that there will be no meeting until that time.

Formal convocation of committees by respective chairmen can scarcely be anticipated until during or after Saar Council.The eighty-fourth session of the Council of the League of Nations was held January 11–21, the Saar plebiscite taking place on January 13. However, in line with above information, it seems safe for WilsonHugh R. Wilson, American Minister in Switzerland and head of the United States delegation to the Disarmament Conference. to arrive here by February 1st.
